sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 70

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 70:1
items.html: (to the question) For the Klipot [shells] take hold only in the ending forces of Kedusha [holiness]. And since the Parsa between Atzilut and BYA [Beria, Yetzira, Assiya] is the place of the boundary of the second Tzimtzum [restriction], therefore from there downward there is a structure of the Klipot, which suckle from the Lights that glimmer below the Tzimtzum, in the secret of Nehiru Dakik [a thin glow], as is known. But above the Parsa, there is the discernment of the line of Ein Sof, blessed be He, and "He, His life-force, and His organs are one in them." (There)
